title: Bloom Energy's 130% Revenue Explosion Is Too Expensive, But Impressive
text: Bloom Energy delivered exceptional Q1 2026 earnings with EPS of $0.44 crushing consensus estimate of $0.1285, a 242% beat. Product revenue surged 208% year-over-year to $653.3 million, while total revenue climbed 130%. Despite impressive growth, the company trades at 128 times forward earnings and 80 times book value, representing expensive valuation relative to industrial peers. The company's transformation into an AI power provider is materializing with management raising full-year 2026 guidance substantially: revenue $3.4B-$3.8B representing 80% YoY growth at midpoint, non-GAAP EPS $1.85-$2.25, and adjusted EBITDA $650M-$800M. Two major deals anchor growth: a $5 billion collaboration with Brookfield Asset Management and an Oracle partnership involving a 2.8 GW commitment. However, related-party sales to Brookfield JVs reached $373.3 million versus $2.8 million prior year, signaling customer concentration that warrants tracking. With a $20 billion backlog, execution on capacity and hyperscaler conversions remains the critical variable driving shareholder returns.
